pages.raw_text:
MISSOULA, Mont, July 14.-At
least 5000 strangers are lodged In
hotels and rooming houses OF this
city today In anticipation of the
opening to the filing on the Flat
head Indian reservation lands to-
morrow. 1t Is expected that $4,000
will register.


The lands OF the Faltheads COM
prise 100000 acres all told. After
the registration a drawing will be
conducted by a committee to be
appointed by the government.


Miss Christie Donlan, aged -,
daughter Of Senator Donlan, has
officially been chosen to draw the
allotments.


Every train brings hundreds of
persons and already tents are
pitched on vacant lots. BY Thurs
day the place will resemble an
army encampment. Temporary
buildings have been erected as
sleeping quarters.


By special order, the city admin
istration has issued a mandate re-
straining the operations of fakirs
during the registration period. Spe-
cial deputies have been added to
the force under Sheriff Graham.
